sentences.text: Great.
Hey, how's it going?
Not too bad, how you doing?
Not too bad.
Hey, Kelly.
Hi, Austin!
How are you?
I'm doing really well, how've you been?
Good!
Hey, Bridget.
Hi, Austin, it's nice to meet you in person.
I feel like we've been emailing.
back and forth a bunch.
Yeah, likewise.
For all your help, as always.
Yeah, no problem.
I felt the same.
I was like, oh, that's funny timing that we're jumping on a call just.
Yeah.
Today, actually.
Then we got… Is it… Mike, Erica… Alan, it's nice to meet you all.
You as well.
Nice to meet you.
There's a few more coming in.
I don't know, Kelly, how… how many folks you've….
I invited the world to Austin.
Awesome.
Excited to see what… hear what the updates are.
Yeah, we're excited to… to share them with you.
What's, ….
I guess, happy to show the updates, but I guess what's been….
New on the AEW side.
Yeah, I'm….
Pretty, pretty business as usual, … We've been… very busy, so that's a good thing.
Yeah.

And always looking to, you know, as our past conversations have gone, always looking to, try and stay on top of the new technologies, and so, you know, it's been really exciting to see Your technologies, growing so quickly, and we've certainly, for those of us who use ApartmentIQ, have certainly enjoyed the product so far, so….
Well, that's awesome, appreciate Okay.
I guess as far as kind of, like, happy to show you the big update would be our, new update to our Explore Premium.

So this is gonna be kind of a main, kind of directly, like, if you're still using, kind of, like, familiar with CoStar, and axiometrics, it should be very similar, in my humble opinion, a little bit easier to understand, too.
So, … that'll be the big update.

I know that, like, Bridget, and then Kelly, yourself, and maybe a few others, if you've seen a few of the updates just with the market survey portion, we've had a few new views to the floor plan and market survey itself, so I can briefly touch on that, but I think as far as, kind of like.
for this group's audience, I think the Explore Premium would be something, just because you don't have access to that currently.
because… and it seems like most of you probably have at least some sort of read-only, like, I know, obviously, like, Bridget, I know you do, but Kelly, does anybody else kind of have I think we're.
They're all read-only.
Okay.
For the most part.
Yeah, and that's what it would be, just kind of through your PMCs.
Yeah.
So, happy to kind of show you there.
I guess, as far as kind of, like, non-PMC access to tools, do you… is it mainly just CoStar or, like, Axiometrics?
I guess, kind of, what… any updates there as far as, like, what tools internally you're using?
I think it's just CoStar… somebody else can correct me if I'm wrong, but I don't think we are using Axiometrics?
At this point.
I think that's about… right?
Does anyone know if we're… we're not still using Axiometrics, right?
I think that went away in this last year.
Yeah.
Okay, so just CoStar, and, so I'll be happy to go through that.
… Let me just share my screen here quickly.
Then we also had… if you guys have met… I think you've met Justin from our team before, ….
You look familiar, so nice to see everybody again.
You too.

So as far as a quick update, I think you'll see, we've now actually kind of, separated out on the market survey just calculated metrics versus property summary, so it's nice and easy to just kind of quickly see which is going to be more calculated and which is just, you know, straight rent, concessions.
Separated that out a little bit nicer.
A few of the filters, too, we've now added a bill-to-rent, filter, so that's very, depending on the markets that you're in, I know folks like to filter by that.

And then the floor plan report, I think, is our most heavily used report, especially from, … a lot of folks kind of, like, in your position like this, just because you can quickly… if you haven't seen this report, you can quickly use our historical data, with current performance.
It's just nice to see how you've been performing by floor plan over any sort of time period in here.
You also get the nice release breakdowns.
But as far as, kind of, what I wanted to show y'all is the Explore Premium.

So, … right in here, if you guys were to have this internally, you obviously would have then all of your AEW properties under the My Properties, so you wouldn't have to toggle through, all the different PMCs, you wouldn't have to worry about getting access, Bridget.
you would have everything right here, and then in addition, you would have the Explore Premium.
So in here, it would be nationwide access.
You can type in any, neighborhood, zip code, city, whatever you want to search by, so I'll just type in Dallas, for instance.
What you get in here is a real-time snapshot, so you get rent and performance, you get year-over-year rent growth.
This is back to Q1 of 23.
You get 30-day current pricing snapshots, so it's gonna show your asking rent, effective rent, concessions, then all broken out by floor plan.
You get new construction supplies, so this is gonna be under construction, scheduled, and in planning.
Deliveries by quarter, Then we've also included demographic information.
So you can see… Population, income, household, age.
And then a very interactive map, so you can actually draw or make a radius if you want to look at a specific area.
And then once you select that area, the snapshots, … your boundaries will update as is.
So I hit this, and now your deliveries by quarter and everything will populate for just that selected area.
… Any ques… as far as, kind of, like, your workflow within CoStar, is this… kind of matching up with what you guys generally use?
I just would like to kind of know, like, how, you know, often are you in CoStar?
What are you normally, you know, leveraging within there?
I think… And I think you said that you guys were working on it, but the, sale data?
Yeah.
and market reports.
I think the other thing, too, is the new supply, as well, is a really helpful one.
Okay.
Being able to, like, toggle around… which I think you guys have, that CoStar… a little bit hard… less user-friendly on Coaster, where you can toggle around on a map.
And look at the new supply.
Yeah, that's so nice.
Yeah, that's a good call out, Jenna.
So you can totally do that with the new supply, so you could just view all the new supply, and this toggles just to the new supply.
And then it's… all the pins, then, are pop-up boxes, so you can actually see what the call for construction details are for each individual pin.
… And then, ….
Would you, when you're doing that, can you, toggle off and say, you know.
Sort that data, so it's a minimum of 100 units, for example.
Yeah, good call out.
So, right here, ….
Got it.
We have all of our filters.
Yep.
Unit count is definitely one of them.
You can do… and then you're built, you can filter by management companies, so you can see how certain management companies are doing versus others.
Property class, construction status, I mean, really, all the filters that you're really after in here.
… Oh, okay, so could you see, for example, all of… not just New Supply, but all of the Gray Star Managed properties in that market?
Yep, absolutely.
That's cool.
It's amazing.

We have some… we have some clients that if they're, … going into a market they're not super familiar with, they'll kind of use just the market overview as a baseline, and then kind of take a look at their top 3 potential management partners, and just kind of see who's performing the best, so… Yeah, a nice little way to compare.
Wow.
That can be really dangerous.
sad.
We're doing acquisitions.

Yeah, it's really handy and slick, … Then, as I toggled over here to summar… properties from the market summary, then we have, like you mentioned, Kelly, this is where you can get that transactional and ownership data, and then also pull just a new report if you wanted for just that property.
So if you just popped into, like, Strata here, you'll get the ownership information, management information.
all your building specs, and then this is honestly just kind of like its own little market survey right within.
You get broken out by floor plan, concessions, exposure.
We'll show you the comps.
You can just go ahead and generate a new report from right in here.
… gives you that kind of a little market survey, like I mentioned, breakdown.
Transactional data would be loaded right in here.
This is just the test environment.
Then you would have a breakdown of all the nearby properties and new supply.
You can change that radius, so you can toggle between how far you wanted to look out for new supply by that property.
And then you also have all of the demographic information at the bottom.
What's the source for the… Sale transaction data.
Justin, you got that?
Yeah, we partnered with, RCA.
So, Row Capital Analytics.
Yep.

Yeah, I think what's nice about that is we've… Kind of in that… partnership process, we… I don't want to say interview, but we… we took demos with quite a few, you know, vendors out there, and we found that they had the best.

So, that's who we went with, and like Austin said, this is kind of like a… it's a QA process right now, it's going to be launched at the end of the month for everybody, but in addition to all that information, we're also going to have property, unit, and amenity-specific images for the property.
Cap rates will be included in there.
And, ownership, or I'm sorry, transactional history will go all the way back to 2002, and it's going to include everything over $2.
5 million.
So it should be, you know, coverage should be pretty good there.
Oh, great.
Yeah.
From here, then, go ahead, did you have something, Kelly?
Alright, one more question.
Yeah, so I know that you were starting on multifamily.
Are you moving to other sector types as well?
BTR's the big one right now.
Yep.
So, we have… We're calling it the largest definitive BTR database in the entire industry.
So we're very, very confident in our BTR database.
In this same release, for this, this new feature release, we're gonna have affordable and senior and student, in here as well, so you'll be able to toggle right on the map.
For Affordable Senior Student.
There is single-family home included in here already.
So, you can track single-family homes, … I'm curious, any other kind of…
Are you looking for anything in particular, I guess?
I don't know if you're also going to be eventually growing this out to, or at least this section out to office, retail, industrial type.

Not right now, and I think… I'll let you know, I mean, as far as, like, the… some of the groups we've been working with that have been kind of piloting this, what's nice is they said, like, hey, like, this actually can replace CoStar, so they didn't renew their CoStar agreements, a handful of them.

But those groups that still need Office Industrial, obviously there's no workaround for that right now, and honestly, it's not on our immediate roadmap by any means, so I think from a CoStar perspective, you probably still need to have it for that space.
Yep.
Can you… Can you talk a little bit more on the SFR?
Side, and how you're tracking that, and is that just gonna be, institutional owners?
Or portfolios, or are you also going to be tracking the mom-and-pop rentals as well?
Yeah, so it'll have to be, … there'll have to be an online presence, and that could be, like, … an Apartments.
com or Homes.
com is.
Right.
like that.
If it's on Craigslist, or someone putting up, like, … you know, a Facebook post, we're not going to be able to track them.
So, I don't want to say only institutional, but there'll have to be some kind of, like, an online presence with, an institutional company, like I said, like an Apartments.
com or a Homes.
com or something like that.
Yeah, and when I say institutional, I mean on the ownership side.
Right.
Yeah.
Not on… not as much on… yeah.
Okay.
That'll be interesting to… to see.
Since we're… we're now active in that space, and we've been relying on a partner who has their own you know, built-out software, but I think that This could be a… Complimentary software to that.
For sure, yeah, and that's one of the big things, too.
I mean, I'd say… if you're using Explore here, even if you're looking to edit comps in, like, the market survey section, that map's gonna include absolutely everything.
So it'll include those single-family homes, it'll include duplexes, fourplexes, so use those filters.
I mean, it's, you know.
you know, take away some of the noise there, use the asset class, total units, whatever it might be.
But yeah, because that's gonna… it's going to include everything.
Yeah, and then the last portion on there, then, too, you'd have access to then the individual, Kelly, you mentioned earlier, then the individual research reports.
So then, when you're looking at these new deals, whether it's an existing property or even a new development.
all of your deals will live right here under the research section, and then anytime you came back to that deal to look at it, it's gonna be updated for that day.
So, always having live data for all your projects, and it's as easy as just hitting new report, typing in either the property name or address.
So this is one I was just working on a partner with.
creates that market survey within seconds.
You can have that same ability.
This looks exactly like the market surveys for an existing property.
Again, to Justin's point, you can come in here, edit your comps however you wish.
You can use all the filters that we have at your disposal.
And then just go ahead and export this to, you know, Excel or PDF.
That's great.
Austin, in Explore, can you show the market comparison tab?
I think that might be valuable.
Yeah, let me jump over there.
So, as Justin mentioned in here, too, you can… Create your top markets to just compare them at a high-level glance.
So you can quickly see all your different MSAs, price per square foot, rents, concessions, lease percentage, days on market, new supply.
All that just kind of in a nice high-level overview as well.

Yeah, like, this example right here for the demo account, we're just showing the top 50 MSAs in the country, but if you want, you can break this out by zip codes in Dallas or neighborhoods in Phoenix.
I mean, you can actually pick and choose or define what you consider a market or sub-market.
So, really nice to kind of keep an eye on these different areas to see what might look right for acquisition or for development.
And you can pick and choose your different timeframes in here as well.
So, like, right now we're showing this data over the last 30 days, but you can go all the way back to the last 4 years.
And then there is some of the ability to hide or remove some of the, the columns there.
I mean, there's quite a few data points in here, so you can customize it a little bit.
Any forecasting capabilities?
It'll be out at the end of the month.
Yeah, so we actually hired one of CoStar's, economists about 2-3 months ago.
Apparently he was one of their big dogs, they weren't very happy that we took him.
But that was… that was actually the first thing that he started on, whenever he joined the company.
So, the very first rollout of that will be coming, at the end of this month.
And, more to follow, so we're really, really excited about that.
That's probably the… on the roadmap after ownership grouping, all this transactional information, that's probably our most requested feature, so that's a priority.
You guys are just… Conquering the world.
Fire.
We're trying to.

But yeah, like I said, I mean, everybody we talk to, everyone's marrying, like, 3-4 different data sources together, trying to make sense of it, so… I think we have the best rent comp data in the industry right now, so it's more so just kind of, you know, completing the picture with forecasting, with ownership information, with your, you know, your transactional data, so….
Wow.
And then, I see revenue management on here, are you still working on that?
tool.
I think you were in testing last time we've… Talked?
Yeah, it actually fully went live last month.
Okay.
So, something to also be on the radar, it's called Daylight.
… Industry's first fully, powered by fully public data, so, for obvious reasons, it's been, very popular so far.

Yeah, if you want to click into it real quick, Austin, I can just give them, like, a quick 2-second on it, but I think what's nice about this, and the whole deal with, like, Arum and, you know, with RealPage, is they had to, you know, kind of turn off that database that had all that private information.
What's great with our approach, with, you know, tracking this unit-level data for the last 40, er, for the last, for the last 4 or 5 years, is we essentially have the public version of that.
So all of this data that we have is what's going into our rev management tool.
But from here, it's a pretty similar layout to the market survey, like, each one of your properties gets its own card, and then everything in here is optimized for occupancy.
So you set a rate, an occupancy rate, and the time frame you want to hit it, and then the system responds with whether or not you'll sign enough leases to make up for turnover and exposure.
So, like, Austin, if you click into… yeah, just click on Eastern Arbor Apartments right there.
Just, like, kind of click… and I'm not there, just, like, the tab.
There you go, that's good, that's good.
So, like, this, for example, like, if we look at studios.
Our occupancy target for the next 84 days is gonna be 92%.
Right now, we're sitting at 88%, which is a 2 lease shortfall.

And the system's saying that if we don't change anything for our studios, then in 84 days, our studios are going to sit at 78, or sorry, 76%, which is going to be an 8-lease shortfall of our studio… of our occupancy target.
So what we're saying is that our system's recommending taking the base rates of our studios from $12.
20 a month down to $11.
32 a month.
And what's great about this is, Austin, if you click on the 1132 there.
we actually used what we call explainable AI to tell you exactly why the system's making these recommendations.

So we've heard that a lot of teams, you know, Aram, all these pricing tools, they make their pricing recommendations, then you sit there for 30 minutes, an hour, trying to figure out why they're making that recommendation.
Well, right here, we tell you exactly why.

So for this instance, the highest driving factor is that application velocity is lower than exposure, but also a few units have near-term availability, asking rents are higher than leased rents, and then also studios are sitting on the mark for longer than you'd like.
So these four factors are actually what's going into, you know, our system making this recommendation.
How does that account for, upfront concessions?
So everything we base it off of is coming off of the net effective rent.
So we're taking all net effective numbers here in terms of the recommendations we're making.
So, it's taking what's transacted in terms of net effect difference, and then is it… but it's not recommending any upfront concessions.
It's not.
So, does your rent then… Start to trend downward as a result?

So, every… every… also, if you can click out of this, or… if you go to the available… yeah, so every rent we're making here would be based off… all the data we're taking into account, so all the available units, all the lease data that's influencing our model is based off a net… off of a net effective number.
And then this price right here, like the 11… I'm sorry, the $12.
20 for studios, that's based off of the base rent for your studios, and then we do have the ability in the settings to set your floor plan and amenity premiums.
But we're never going to recommend a concession for you.
We're just saying this is the actual rent you need to get to in order to reach your goal.
Got it.

Yeah, and what's cool, too, is, I mean, the transparency factors, like, Austin, if you click back into the 1132, you can see there's, like, 4 different tabs here across the top, but you can look at the trends.
So I think, like, post, post-pandemic, we're back to some normalized seasonality or trends, so you can look at the trends there.
You can look at market position, which is… is nice.
… If you go back to the top, Austin, where it says Available Units, if you click into there, this is where we'll show you the details for the 13 studios that are available.
So at the top, you'll get, like, an exposure forecasting here.
at the bottom, you'll see the actual units, the 13 studios that are available, and this is actually the fully baked price.
Like, the recommended rent there that's highlighted, that's the fully baked pricing, including your amenity and, and, floor plan premiums.
But Austin, if you click into the 1289, or 1287, any of those, we'll actually give you the, the pricing matrix.
So part of the customization that you have the ability for is setting your term lengths.
Down below here, you can account for your grace period.
So, you know, if you want to give them a week until you start realizing revenue, you can do that as well.
And then, this is all actually being powered by the expiration curve, which is another report that you have to open up in the background.
But… That's… that's the high-level 36.
Yeah, yeah.
Been really, really well received.

I think, you know, where it says select Rent Austin, you know, if you want to approve it, you can approve it, it'll push it back into your system, so we have the integration set up with Yardi, and Toronto will be finished in the next 30 days, and then with RealPage, they're not playing as nice, but we'll get it done.
I'm sure.
Who do you guys use, out of curiosity?
For revenue management?
For… I'm sorry, for your property management software.
So our, our… PMCs use… Everything.
Okay, cool.
But… You're already one site.
Cool.
Yeah.
We also have Entradas.
Yeah, I'd say if you guys would want to trial it out, happy to kind of set up a pilot, and that would be inclusive as well, or be free as well, but yeah, happy to do that.
See what you think.
It's really interesting.
Guys, Rolled that out quickly from… From our seat.

It was, 18 months of work in the background, and I think what's exciting about it is… is pretty much for the last 3-4 years working with, you know, some of our enterprise customers, it's like, hey, we have to use ApartmentIQ to verify everything with these pricing calls.
Like, you guys have the data, like, build something for us.
Right, right.
So over the last 18 months, like, I think it's, like, 7 of the top 10 or 7 of the top 12 have influenced us and kind of almost built the product roadmap with us.
So I think it's a lot of what everybody's looking for.
… you know, renewals are baked into it, it's, … I don't know, it's been… it's been really well received.
So, it's, … it's taken off.
I think we, like Austin said, I think we set it live, or set up I mean, officially made it up for sale on July 3rd.
In the first week, we did, like, 30 demos.
So, it seems like people were kind of sick of the legacy products.
Obviously, there's some newer games in town, but at the end of the day, I think we have that public database that made Arum so successful.
Right.
Okay.
Something to think about.
Yeah, I guess as far as, … kind of, like, keeping the conversation going, would it seem like, obviously, the Explore would be highly useful?
Revenue management, I guess, where… where do you think you guys kind of think….
Explorer would definitely… I mean, I… completely see the value there.
It's just that, … That's a product where… It needs to be our corporate budget, right?
And, …
You were along for the ride with me on that one, like, that's….
Not as easy.
To get done, and we already have CoStar, right, because we're… we are across multi-sectors, so, I don't think CoStar readily available, readily offers us to peel off Multifamily, right?
They're probably not going to make that so easy.
Right.
… So, from that perspective, that's… that's the roadblock that we've been encountering on that piece of it.
On the revenue management side of things, …
you know, we have most of… I would say most of our products that are… properties that are using revenue management are in the RealPage products.
… you know, we've been trying to test a couple of the other ones.
I've personally tested one of the, other systems starting at the end of last summer, and we're about to move it over, because it just was too clunky.
… So, I wish you guys had this a year ago, so I could have just tested this right away, but, …
You know, it's definitely something that's… That we need to stay on top of, just in light of all of the… those… Discussions.
Right.
Yeah, I guess as far as kind of, like, like, do you guys normally budget on, like, a calendar year, or is it just kind of… Different, I guess.
Good question.
I think so?
You mean for the, for the, the co-star, like.
Right.
Yeah, like, obviously….
4, whatever.
Yeah, like, if… obviously, you wouldn't just have Explorer, like, rolled out tomorrow, but ….
Right.
Like, what… just generally speaking, like, when you do do, you know, softwares or tools to that nature.
above my pay grade, Austin, but, … I would imagine it's calendar year.
Okay.
Yeah.
And maybe we should also, you know, we had some turnover in our research team, and I think that you had talked to Adrian at one point.
Yeah.
But, so, I might want to get you in front of the The current team, as well.
Yeah, I'd be happy to.
Just to get on their radar.
Yeah, certainly.
Cause yeah, we can… we're pretty flexible as far as, like, billing start dates and aligning with those timelines.
Right.
Yeah, definitely would be happy to… to meet with them, and then just kind of see where… where things would go from there, if that seems like the next best step.
Yeah.
Don't get your hopes up, because, you know, I took you along for a bumpy ride last time, but….
All… all good, it's….
Yeah.
Like Justin mentioned, I mean, like, we work with a lot of these companies for, you know, a year plus, so I totally understand it doesn't happen overnight.
Yep.
Dope.
Got it.
Okay, super helpful to see where you guys are.
It was really actually helpful to get the revenue management, overview as well, because that's something that we need to stay on top of.
… And then just to see where the new… where the new, features are within ApartmentIQ, because we're all on that every single day, I think.
Those of us who have it, so… Yeah, it's great to see that you guys use it so much.
Yeah.
Well, great.
I can follow up then just with, like, some updated information.
Sure.
Maybe just kind of loop me in with the investment team, is that…?
Yep.
Sound good?
Yep.
Yep, that sounds great.
Awesome.
I appreciate it.
And it was, good seeing everybody.
Thank you.
Thank you, Austin.
Thank you, Justin.
Thank you, Austin.
Thank you.
Hey, Carrie.
